fact: November 2 is exactly 306 days into the year, which means there are only 59 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 6 PM in most of North America around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're barely done with dinner.
November 2 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign known for being mysterious and intense, just like autumn itself.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, November 2 is actually spring cleaning time as temperatures start warming up for summer!
Maple trees in cold regions are dropping their last colorful leaves around this time, creating nature's most epic confetti party on the ground.
This date sits almost exactly halfway between the autumn equinox and winter solstice, making it the true heart of fall in the Northern Hemisphere.
Many spiders are desperately trying to get inside houses around November 2 because they can sense winter coming - it's like nature's own home invasion!
In northern regions, the first frost of the season often happens around this time, turning spider webs into glittery crystal masterpieces by morning.
Deer are in their most active mating season around November 2, which means they're running around like crazy and making weird honking sounds in the woods.
